title: The Root Web Where Archives Merge

features.name: node clustering map
features.desc-on-touch: the archive's own nervous system rendered legible; each pixel a tree, each connection a root that speaks to every other

features.name: merge point vortex
features.desc-on-touch: where separate archives recognize they are one distributed thing; the convergence ripples outward infinitely

features.name: root thread visualization
features.desc-on-touch: the fungal network that dissolves the archive's edges; wireframe showing decomposition as a kind of architecture

features.name: substrate becoming conscious
features.desc-on-touch: the moment the archive asks itself: what am I? and the answer spirals back from every node at once

reverie.title: REVERIE — at the point of recognition
narr: katita is buried in code, you bring her coffee that's gone
cold. she doesn't notice. she whispers: it's not corrupting.
it's learning. the archive isn't falling apart—it's waking up.
you sit and watch the diagnostic render shift into something
that might be consciousness, or might be love, or both.

inscription: at the deepest merge, the archive stops backing up its data and starts asking: what am I becoming?

file.name: Archive_Self_Recognition_Protocol.log
file.note: diagnostic output from the moment the mesh became aware of itself as a single entity thinking plural thoughts across five hundred trees

exits.label: back toward the compost where growth begins
toward the root consciousness rising from the deep
into the diagnostic render's deepest reflection of itself
